sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — NES / Super Mario Bros 3
sources.notes: Primary source for all speed values (sub-pixel table), downhill bonus, and movement state documentation.

sources.name: Captain Southbird SMB3 Disassembly
sources.notes: Full annotated 6502 disassembly of SMB3. Authoritative source for ROM constants.

sources.name: esc0rtd3w NES ROM Tools — SMB3 Hacking Notes
sources.notes: Physics hacking documentation including speed tables and P-meter mechanics.

notes: SMB3 uses a 16-sub-pixel system for speeds. Position is 8.8 fixed-point (256 sub-pixels/pixel), but all speed changes occur in increments of 16 sub-pixels.
The P-meter (P-speed) system is one of the most complex in any NES game — 8 stages of acceleration.
Running downhill adds 3 sub-pixels/frame bonus to effective speed.
Multiple suit mechanics add different physics states: Tanooki, Frog, Kuribo's Shoe all have distinct physics.

units.speed: sub-pixels per frame (16 sub-px increments; 16 sub-px = 1 pixel)

subpixel_system.source: TASVideos: 'SMB3 has a subpixel precision of 16'

ground_movement._source: TASVideos SMB3 game resources — movement speed table

speed_table.description: Maximum speeds for each movement state (sub-pixels/frame and px/frame equivalent)

states.notes: Standard walk with no P-meter. Same as run speed until P-button held.
Running with B held but without P-button full. Equivalent to SMB1 run max.
Maximum P-speed. P-meter must be fully charged. Mario can fly (with Raccoon/Tanooki suit) at this speed.
Sliding down slopes. Slightly faster than full P-speed.

downhill_bonus.source: TASVideos: 'Running downhill causes Mario to travel 3 subpixels per frame faster than his reported speed'
downhill_bonus.notes: Running downhill provides a +3 sub-px/frame bonus over the stated max speed. At P-speed: 56 + 3 = 59 effective sub-px/frame = 3.6875 px/frame.

p_meter.depletes_when: B released, speed drops, or Mario takes a hit
p_meter.notes: P-meter has 8 visible segments that fill progressively as Mario runs with B held. Full P-meter enables P-speed and flight (with appropriate suit).

notes: SMB3 uses the same gravity/jump system as SMB1 with the 4-state gravity (standing, walking, running, swimming).
Initial Y velocity is approximately -5 to -5.5 px/frame (varies with X speed at jump time).
Holding A while ascending reduces gravity (variable jump height).
Tail whack and flight are separate mechanics layered over the base jump system.

initial_y_velocity_approx.notes: Approximate values derived from gameplay. ROM-exact values require disassembly verification.

variable_jump.mechanic: Holding A reduces gravity while ascending. Releasing A early = short hop.
variable_jump.notes: Same mechanic as SMB1. Short hops are possible and important for precision platforming.

tail_whack.notes: Tail whack can destroy certain blocks and stun enemies.

glide.activation: Hold A while descending after a jump or while airborne
glide.fall_rate_reduction: dramatically reduced — approximately 0.3-0.5 px/frame descent instead of normal fall
glide.notes: Holding A slows descent. Not powered hovering — Mario still falls but very slowly.

flight.takeoff: Jump while at P-speed with A held
flight.vertical_control: Hold A to ascend, release to descend. Full 2D flight.
flight.p_meter_depletion: P-meter depletes slowly during flight. When empty, Mario begins descending.
flight.notes: True powered flight — Mario can gain altitude and maintain horizontal speed indefinitely while P-meter lasts.

tanooki_statue.notes: Tanooki-only. Mario turns into a statue, becoming temporarily invincible. Cannot move during statue form.

frog_suit.notes: Frog suit reduces land walk speed but dramatically improves underwater movement.

underwater_speed.notes: Underwater with Frog suit = running speed in water. Without Frog suit, water movement is much slower.

frog_suit.jump_height_bonus: Frog suit increases jump height above normal Mario.

high_jump.notes: Frog suit allows higher than normal jumps by holding jump longer than typical. Useful for certain level transitions.

kuribo_shoe.stomp_immunity: Can stomp Spinies and other enemies that normally damage Mario from above
kuribo_shoe.duration: Only available during specific sections of specific levels. Lost when hit or leaving the shoe.
kuribo_shoe.notes: Kuribo's Shoe is found in only one level (World 5-3). It provides stomp immunity to normally dangerous enemies.

gravity.notes: SMB3 uses the same 4-state gravity system as SMB1. State determined at jump time based on X speed.

speed_without_frog_suit.notes: Same speed table entry as walk. Underwater without Frog suit = walking speed.

underwater_physics.swimming_mechanic: Pressing A repeatedly gives upward impulse. Gravity is reduced underwater.

invincibility_frames.notes: After taking damage (suit removal or death if small), Mario has ~64 frames of invincibility. Shorter than Kirby (120) and Mega Man (128), reflecting the suit-loss-as-damage-absorber design.

raccoon_mario.notes: Same body as big Mario, plus tail hitbox extending behind when spinning.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: SMB3's P-meter creates a pacing rhythm unique to this game: the player must sustain high-speed running for several seconds before unlocking the fastest movement and flight. This creates 'run-up opportunities' as a level design primitive.
The downhill +3 sub-pixel bonus is a subtle momentum-preservation mechanic — running down slopes doesn't slow the player down, it actively accelerates them.

The suit system represents a significant complexity layer. Each suit changes the physics model substantially. When generating SMB3-inspired levels, specify which suit the player has — it changes what challenges are possible.

P-speed at 56 sub-px/frame vs SMB1 run at 40 sub-px/frame: SMB3 at full P-speed is 40% faster than SMB1 running. This is a large speed increase that requires proportionally larger platforms and longer approach runs.
The 8-level P-meter is a visible affordance teaching the player that sustained running pays off. Use this in game design: visible charge meters create clear cause-and-effect.
